title: Benkara forrestii

intro: Benkara forrestii, also known as Benkara or Indian wild jasmine, is a flowering plant native to the Himalayas and Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Benkara forrestii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions, infusions, and powders, often used in traditional medicine to soothe respiratory and gastrointestinal issues, as well as to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
